item.title: Watermelons, Seedless Giant
item.description: A larger-than-life specimen of Citrullus lanatus, engineered to forgo the traditional reproductive encumbrances, resulting in an unbroken expanse of juicy grandeur. Best appreciated when viewed from a low angle, where its ground-level dominance becomes manifest.

item.title: Giant Watermelon, Sugar Baby
item.description: A cultivar of Citrullus lanatus known for its rotundity and deep green rind with dark stripes, often displayed with pride at county fair weigh-ins. Its posterior, viewed astern, defies casual description.

item.title: Savoy Cabbage
item.description: A leafy vegetable of distinguished girth, characterized by its densely-packed, undulating leaves and a verdant hue that shifts subtly in raking light.

item.title: Combine Harvester, John Deere
item.description: A robust agricultural machine manufactured by John Deere, characterized by its substantial rear housing and impressive grain capacity, especially when viewed from behind in flattering sunlight.

item.title: Tractor, Compact Utility
item.description: A four-wheeled agricultural vehicle of moderate horsepower, built low and wide for orchard work, hobby farms, and the kind of unspecified yard task that a homeowner believes requires diesel.

item.title: Sequoia, General Sherman
item.description: A giant sequoia tree renowned for its extraordinary mass and height, particularly when viewed from ground level where the sky becomes a mere afterthought.

item.title: Pumpkin, Mammoth Gold
item.description: An especially large cultivar of pumpkin renowned for its substantial mass and radiant hues; typically exhibited with pride at autumnal gatherings and agricultural fairs.

item.title: Silo, Grain Concrete
item.description: A towering agricultural structure characterized by its substantial cylindrical form and ability to outshine any pastoral landscape with its commanding presence.

item.title: Cabbage, Savoy
item.description: A botanical heavyweight of the brassica family, renowned for its deeply crinkled leaves and substantial presence in the garden; esp. one whose verdant layers suggest a rotational symmetry best admired in morning light.

item.title: Spruce, Suburban
item.description: A mature evergreen specimen (genus Picea) cultivated as a residential or municipal landscape feature; characteristically pyramidal, with branches sweeping low to the ground in a dense conical mass.

item.title: Watermelon, Moon and Stars
item.description: A cultivar of watermelon renowned for its celestial patterning of yellow spots and larger orbs set against a dark green rind, reminiscent of a night sky.

item.title: Banana, Cavendish
item.description: A cultivar of banana (Musa acuminata) renowned for its robust and curvaceous physique, often found lounging in fruit bowls and breakfast spreads across the globe.

item.title: Avocado, Domestic
item.description: A pear-shaped fruit (Persea americana) of disproportionate top-to-bottom mass distribution, often photographed sliced for visual emphasis.

item.title: Heritage Tomato
item.description: A cultivar of tomato, often grown from seeds passed down through generations, characterized by its robust flavor and generous proportions.

item.title: Pumpkin, Atlantic Giant
item.description: A cultivar of pumpkin renowned for its prodigious girth, cultivated primarily for competitive size and decorative impact.

item.title: Squash, Banana
item.description: A member of the Cucurbitaceae family, characterized by an elongated shape and a robust girth, particularly when viewed in profile against contrasting backgrounds. Often found lounging in suburban kitchen fruit bowls, it demands a second glance.

item.title: Tire, Tractor Rear
item.description: A formidable rubber component affixed to the rear axle of agricultural machinery, designed to convey a sense of unyielding stability and mass, particularly when observed from behind where its full breadth is undeniable.

item.title: Courgette, Giant Green
item.description: A specimen of the Cucurbita pepo species displaying astonishing lateral expansion, often challenging the structural integrity of standard garden beds.
